title: Thikse Monastery
tagline: Thikse Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Thiksey Monastery or Thiksey Gompa (also transliterated from Ladakhi as Thikse, Thiksay or Tikse) is a Buddhist monastery affiliated with the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism.
overview.description: Thiksey Monastery or Thiksey Gompa (also transliterated from Ladakhi as Thikse, Thiksay or Tikse) is a Buddhist monastery affiliated with the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism. It is located on top of a hill in Thiksey approximately 19 kilometres (12 mi) east of Leh, in the Ladakh region of northern India. It is noted for its resemblance to the Potala Palace in Lhasa, Tibet, and is the largest monastery in central Ladakh, notably containing a separate set of buildings for female renunciates that has been the source of significant recent building and reorganization. topPlaces.name: Thiksey
topPlaces.description: Thiksey is a village and the headquarters of its eponymous block in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Chuchot Gongma
topPlaces.description: Chuchot Gongma is a village-group and the headquarter of Chuchot block in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Shey
topPlaces.description: Shey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 15 km from Leh towards Hemis.

topPlaces.name: Shey Monastery
topPlaces.description: Shey Monastery or Gompa or the Shey Palace are complex structures located on a hillock in Shey, 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) to the south of Leh in Ladakh, northern India on the Leh-Manali road. Shey was the summer capital of Ladakh in the past.

topPlaces.name: Stakna Monastery
topPlaces.description: Stakna Monastery or Stakna Gompa is a Buddhist monastery of the Drugpa sect in Stakna, Leh district, Ladakh, northern India, 21 or 25 kilometres from Leh on the left bank of the Indus River. It was founded in the late 16th century by a Bhutanese scholar and saint, Chosje Modzin.

topPlaces.name: Nang, Leh
topPlaces.description: Nang is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Stakna
topPlaces.description: Stakna is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India, on the banks of the Indus River. The Stakna Monastery is located there.

topPlaces.name: Matho Monastery
topPlaces.description: Matho Monastery, or Matho Gonpa or Mangtro Monastery or Mangtro Gonpa, from the Tibetan "mang" that means "many" and "tro" that means "happiness", is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery located 26 kilometres southeast of Leh in Ladakh, northern India, on the banks of the Indus River. The village of Matho is located at the mouth of a deep gorge running out of the Zanskar Range and across the Indus.

items.note: Thiksey is a village and the headquarters of its eponymous block in Leh district, Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

Shey is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil, 15 km from Leh towards Hemis.

Shey Monastery or Gompa or the Shey Palace are complex structures located on a hillock in Shey, 15 kilometres (9.3 mi) to the south of Leh in Ladakh, northern India on the Leh-Manali road. Shey was the summer capital of Ladakh in the past.

Stakna Monastery or Stakna Gompa is a Buddhist monastery of the Drugpa sect in Stakna, Leh district, Ladakh, northern India, 21 or 25 kilometres from Leh on the left bank of the Indus River. It was founded in the late 16th century by a Bhutanese scholar and saint, Chosje Modzin.
Nang is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.
Stakna is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India, on the banks of the Indus River. The Stakna Monastery is located there.

Matho Monastery, or Matho Gonpa or Mangtro Monastery or Mangtro Gonpa, from the Tibetan "mang" that means "many" and "tro" that means "happiness", is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery located 26 kilometres southeast of Leh in Ladakh, northern India, on the banks of the Indus River. The village of Matho is located at the mouth of a deep gorge running out of the Zanskar Range and across the Indus.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thikse Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thikse Monastery is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thikse Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (279 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thikse Monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thikse Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thikse Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Thikse Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thikse Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thiksey, Chuchot Gongma, Shey, Shey Monastery, Stakna Monastery.
